thesis_status_reasoning: All critical parameters demonstrate the thesis is performing exactly as designed during recovery from an asymmetric load event. Sector 4 pressure (38.5 PSI) maintains safe margin above the 20 PSI contamination threshold. Primary pumps have successfully restarted at conservative 30% load while gravity reserves (65%) provide supplemental capacity, demonstrating the intended hybrid resilience model. No falsification criteria are threatened.
action_reasoning: The system is executing its designed fail-safe recovery protocol successfully. The operator log confirms decreasing demand and controlled transition back to normal operations with appropriate reserve buffer remaining.

key_signals.signal: Sector 4 pressure at 38.5 PSI with 18.5 PSI safety margin above critical 20 PSI threshold
Primary pumps restarted at conservative 30% load, avoiding cavitation risk while demand decreases
Gravity reserves at 65% after deployment, demonstrating passive backup functioned and substantial capacity remains
Cavitation sensor reports NORMAL status, confirming no pump damage during event
System flow at 14,000 GPM well below catastrophic leak threshold of 20,000 GPM

biggest_uncertainty: The rate of demand decrease is unquantified - need trend data to confirm recovery trajectory and estimate time to full normal operations
